message:
✨ Add KubeFlex install mission and multi-tenancy setup mission (#1299)

Two new missions for the Multi-Tenancy dashboard:

1. platform-kubeflex.json — Install and configure KubeFlex for
   dedicated control planes per tenant (Helm-based, with create/verify
   control plane steps, uninstall, upgrade, troubleshooting)

2. multi-tenancy-setup.json — Combined framework setup per KubeCon
   Europe 2026 talk "Three Shades of Isolation". Checks which of
   OVN/KubeFlex/K3s/KubeVirt are installed, installs missing ones,
   and configures the full tenant architecture (UDN L2/L3 networks,
   KubeFlex control planes, KubeVirt VMs, K3s server pods)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,192 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "multi-tenancy-setup",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Configure Multi-Tenancy Framework (Three Shades of Isolation)",
+    "description": "Set up the complete multi-tenancy isolation framework combining OVN-Kubernetes, KubeFlex, K3s, and KubeVirt. Based on the KubeCon Europe 2026 talk 'Three Shades of Isolation: A Multi-Tenancy Fortress' by Braulio Dumba & Paolo Dettori (IBM Research). Provides three isolation layers: control-plane (KubeFlex + K3s), data-plane (KubeVirt), and network (OVN UDN L2/L3).",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 45,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check existing components",
+        "description": "Before installing, check which components are already present on the cluster:
```bash
# Check OVN-Kubernetes
kubectl get pods -A -l app=ovnkube-node --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l

# Check KubeFlex
kubectl get pods -A -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflex --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l

# Check KubeVirt
kubectl get pods -A -l app=virt-operator --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l

# Check K3s pods
kubectl get pods -A -l app=k3s --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l
```
> Only install components that are missing (count = 0).",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -A -l app=ovnkube-node --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l",
+          "kubectl get pods -A -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflex --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l",
+          "kubectl get pods -A -l app=virt-operator --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l",
+          "kubectl get pods -A -l app=k3s --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install OVN-Kubernetes with UDN support (Network isolation)",
+        "description": "OVN-Kubernetes provides User Defined Networks (UDN) for Layer 2/3 network tenant isolation.

```bash
# Create OVN-Kubernetes namespace
kubectl create namespace ovn-kubernetes

# Deploy OVN-Kubernetes (adjust version as needed)
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-cni-overlay.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-controller.yaml

# Wait for OVN pods to be ready
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app=ovnkube-node -n ovn-kubernetes --timeout=300s
```
> Skip this step if OVN-Kubernetes is already installed.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl create namespace ovn-kubernetes",
+          "kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-cni-overlay.yaml",
+          "kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-controller.yaml",
+          "kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app=ovnkube-node -n ovn-kubernetes --timeout=300s"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install KubeFlex (Control-plane isolation)",
+        "description": "KubeFlex provides dedicated control planes per tenant.

```bash
helm repo add kubestellar https://kubestellar.github.io/helm
helm repo update
helm install kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex --namespace kubeflex-system --create-namespace

# Wait for controller
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflex -n kubeflex-system --timeout=120s
```
> Skip if KubeFlex is already installed.",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm repo add kubestellar https://kubestellar.github.io/helm",
+          "helm repo update",
+          "helm install kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex --namespace kubeflex-system --create-namespace",
+          "kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflex -n kubeflex-system --timeout=120s"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install KubeVirt (Data-plane isolation)",
+        "description": "KubeVirt runs VMs as Kubernetes pods so tenants don't share compute nodes.

```bash
# Install KubeVirt operator
export KUBEVIRT_VERSION=$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubevirt-prow/release/kubevirt/kubevirt/stable.txt)
kubectl create -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-operator.yaml
kubectl create -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-cr.yaml

# Wait for operator
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app=virt-operator -n kubevirt --timeout=300s
```
> Skip if KubeVirt is already installed.",
+        "commands": [
+          "export KUBEVIRT_VERSION=$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubevirt-prow/release/kubevirt/kubevirt/stable.txt)",
+          "kubectl create -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-operator.yaml",
+          "kubectl create -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-cr.yaml",
+          "kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app=virt-operator -n kubevirt --timeout=300s"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create tenant namespaces and UDN networks",
+        "description": "Set up the network architecture for the first tenant with dual UDN (L3 primary + L2 secondary):

```bash
# Create tenant namespaces
kubectl create namespace tenant-1-ns1
kubectl create namespace tenant-1-ns2

# Label for tenant association
kubectl label namespace tenant-1-ns1 tenant=tenant-1
kubectl label namespace tenant-1-ns2 tenant=tenant-1

# Create Layer-3 UDN (primary — data-plane traffic)
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8s.ovn.org/v1
kind: UserDefinedNetwork
metadata:
  name: tenant-1-l3-primary
  namespace: tenant-1-ns1
spec:
  topology: Layer3
  layer3:
    role: Primary
    subnets:
      - cidr: 10.100.0.0/16
EOF

# Create Layer-2 UDN (secondary — control-plane traffic)
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8s.ovn.org/v1
kind: ClusterUserDefinedNetwork
metadata:
  name: tenant-1-l2-secondary
spec:
  namespaceSelector:
    matchLabels:
      tenant: tenant-1
  network:
    topology: Layer2
    layer2:
      role: Secondary
      subnets:
        - cidr: 10.200.0.0/16
EOF
```
> This creates the dual-network architecture: L3 for data-plane, L2 for control-plane.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl create namespace tenant-1-ns1",
+          "kubectl create namespace tenant-1-ns2",
+          "kubectl label namespace tenant-1-ns1 tenant=tenant-1",
+          "kubectl label namespace tenant-1-ns2 tenant=tenant-1"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create KubeFlex control plane for the tenant",
+        "description": "Provision a dedicated control plane for the tenant:

```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: tenancy.kubeflex.io/v1alpha1
kind: ControlPlane
metadata:
  name: tenant-1
spec:
  type: k8s
  backend: shared
EOF

# Wait for control plane to be ready
kubectl wait --for=jsonpath='{.status.phase}'=Ready controlplane/tenant-1 --timeout=120s
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: tenancy.kubeflex.io/v1alpha1
kind: ControlPlane
metadata:
  name: tenant-1
spec:
  type: k8s
  backend: shared
EOF"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy KubeVirt VM in tenant namespace-1",
+        "description": "Create a KubeVirt VM in namespace-1 with dual NICs (eth0 → L3 UDN primary, eth1 → L2 UDN secondary):

```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1
kind: VirtualMachine
metadata:
  name: tenant-1-vm
  namespace: tenant-1-ns1
  labels:
    tenant: tenant-1
spec:
  running: true
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        tenant: tenant-1
        kubevirt.io/vm: tenant-1-vm
      annotations:
        k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/networks: tenant-1-l2-secondary
    spec:
      domain:
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: 2Gi
        devices:
          interfaces:
            - name: default
              masquerade: {}
            - name: l2-secondary
              bridge: {}
          disks:
            - name: containerdisk
              disk:
                bus: virtio
      networks:
        - name: default
          pod: {}
        - name: l2-secondary
          multus:
            networkName: tenant-1-l2-secondary
      volumes:
        - name: containerdisk
          containerDisk:
            image: quay.io/kubevirt/fedora-cloud-container-disk-demo:latest
EOF
```
> The VM gets eth0 on L3 UDN (data-plane) and eth1 on L2 UDN (control-plane).",
+        "commands": []
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy K3s server pod in tenant namespace-2",
+        "description": "Deploy a K3s server pod connected via KubeFlex controller through the default K8s network and L2 UDN secondary:

```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: k3s-server
  namespace: tenant-1-ns2
  labels:
    app: k3s
    tenant: tenant-1
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: k3s
      tenant: tenant-1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: k3s
        tenant: tenant-1
      annotations:
        k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/networks: tenant-1-l2-secondary
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: k3s-server
          image: rancher/k3s:v1.31.0-k3s1
          command: ["k3s", "server", "--disable=traefik"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6443
              name: api
EOF
```
> K3s gets eth0 on default network (managed by KubeFlex) and eth1 on L2 UDN (control-plane traffic).",
+        "commands": []
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the multi-tenancy stack",
+        "description": "Confirm all three isolation layers are operational:

```bash
echo '=== Control-plane isolation (KubeFlex + K3s) ==='
kubectl get controlplanes
kubectl get pods -n tenant-1-ns2 -l app=k3s

echo '=== Data-plane isolation (KubeVirt) ==='
kubectl get vmi -n tenant-1-ns1

echo '=== Network isolation (OVN UDN) ==='
kubectl get userdefinednetworks -A
kubectl get clusteruserdefinednetworks
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get controlplanes",
+          "kubectl get pods -n tenant-1-ns2 -l app=k3s",
+          "kubectl get vmi -n tenant-1-ns1",
+          "kubectl get userdefinednetworks -A",
+          "kubectl get clusteruserdefinednetworks"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove tenant resources",
+        "description": "Delete tenant VMs, K3s pods, control planes, and namespaces:
```bash
kubectl delete vm --all -n tenant-1-ns1
kubectl delete deployment k3s-server -n tenant-1-ns2
kubectl delete controlplane tenant-1
kubectl delete namespace tenant-1-ns1 tenant-1-ns2
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl delete vm --all -n tenant-1-ns1",
+          "kubectl delete deployment k3s-server -n tenant-1-ns2",
+          "kubectl delete controlplane tenant-1",
+          "kubectl delete namespace tenant-1-ns1 tenant-1-ns2"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "VM not getting L2 secondary network",
+        "description": "**Cause:** The ClusterUserDefinedNetwork may not have matched the namespace labels.

**Fix:** Verify namespace labels match the selector:
```bash
kubectl get namespace tenant-1-ns1 --show-labels
kubectl describe clusteruserdefinednetwork tenant-1-l2-secondary
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "K3s pod not connecting to KubeFlex control plane",
+        "description": "**Cause:** The KubeFlex controller may not have provisioned the control plane service yet.

**Fix:** Check control plane status and K3s logs:
```bash
kubectl get controlplanes
kubectl logs -l app=k3s -n tenant-1-ns2
```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful deployment shows: KubeFlex control plane in Ready state, KubeVirt VM running in tenant-1-ns1, K3s server pod running in tenant-1-ns2, and UDN networks assigned to both namespaces. All three isolation layers (control-plane, data-plane, network) are operational.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get controlplanes",
+        "kubectl get vmi -n tenant-1-ns1",
+        "kubectl get pods -n tenant-1-ns2 -l app=k3s",
+        "kubectl get userdefinednetworks -A"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "multi-tenancy",
+      "isolation",
+      "control-plane",
+      "data-plane",
+      "network",
+      "ovn-kubernetes",
+      "kubeflex",
+      "k3s",
+      "kubevirt",
+      "kubecon",
+      "kubestellar"
+    ],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "multi-tenancy",
+    "platformProvider": "KubeStellar",
+    "supportedK8sVersions": [
+      "1.28",
+      "1.29",
+      "1.30",
+      "1.31"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kubevirt"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "ControlPlane",
+      "VirtualMachine",
+      "UserDefinedNetwork",
+      "ClusterUserDefinedNetwork",
+      "Deployment",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "multi-tenancy"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://kubestellar.io/docs/multi-tenancy/",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kubestellar/kubeflex"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 98
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.28",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "helm",
+      "curl"
+    ],
+    "cloudCLI": "none",
+    "resources": "Minimum 8 vCPU, 16GB RAM (hosting cluster with VMs and nested clusters)",
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster (OpenShift, EKS, GKE, or similar) with sufficient resources for running VMs and nested control planes."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-16T22:00:00.000Z",

@@ -0,0 +1,164 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "platform-kubeflex",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure KubeFlex (Multi-Tenant Control Planes)",
+    "description": "KubeFlex is a KubeStellar project that provides dedicated Kubernetes control planes per tenant. It enables scalable multi-tenant control plane management where each tenant gets their own API server, scheduler, and controller manager — giving a 'vanilla K8s experience' without cluster sprawl.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 10,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install KubeFlex using Helm",
+        "description": "Add the KubeStellar Helm repository and install KubeFlex:\n```bash\nhelm repo add kubestellar https://kubestellar.github.io/helm\nhelm repo update\nhelm install kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex --namespace kubeflex-system --create-namespace\n```\n> This installs the KubeFlex controller and CRDs.",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm repo add kubestellar https://kubestellar.github.io/helm",
+          "helm repo update",
+          "helm install kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex --namespace kubeflex-system --create-namespace"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify KubeFlex controller is running",
+        "description": "Check that the KubeFlex controller pod is running:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubeflex-system\nkubectl get crds | grep kubeflex\n```\n> You should see the kubeflex-controller pod in Running state and ControlPlane CRD registered.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -n kubeflex-system",
+          "kubectl get crds | grep kubeflex"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create a tenant control plane",
+        "description": "Create a dedicated control plane for a tenant:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f - <<EOF\napiVersion: tenancy.kubeflex.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ControlPlane\nmetadata:\n  name: tenant-1\nspec:\n  type: k8s\n  backend: shared\nEOF\n```\n> This creates a virtual control plane named 'tenant-1'.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF\napiVersion: tenancy.kubeflex.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ControlPlane\nmetadata:\n  name: tenant-1\nspec:\n  type: k8s\n  backend: shared\nEOF"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the control plane",
+        "description": "Check that the control plane is provisioned and ready:\n```bash\nkubectl get controlplanes\nkubectl get pods -l kubeflex.io/control-plane=tenant-1\n```\n> The control plane should show as 'Ready' and its pods should be running.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get controlplanes",
+          "kubectl get pods -l kubeflex.io/control-plane=tenant-1"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Access the tenant control plane",
+        "description": "Get the kubeconfig for the tenant control plane:\n```bash\nkubectl get secret tenant-1-admin-kubeconfig -o jsonpath='{.data.kubeconfig}' | base64 -d > tenant-1-kubeconfig.yaml\nKUBECONFIG=tenant-1-kubeconfig.yaml kubectl get nodes\n```\n> This extracts the kubeconfig for direct access to the tenant's API server.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get secret tenant-1-admin-kubeconfig -o jsonpath='{.data.kubeconfig}' | base64 -d > tenant-1-kubeconfig.yaml",
+          "KUBECONFIG=tenant-1-kubeconfig.yaml kubectl get nodes"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Delete all control planes",
+        "description": "Remove all tenant control planes first:\n```bash\nkubectl delete controlplanes --all\n```\n> Wait for all control plane pods to terminate.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl delete controlplanes --all"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall KubeFlex",
+        "description": "Remove the KubeFlex Helm release:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall kubeflex -n kubeflex-system\nkubectl delete namespace kubeflex-system\nkubectl delete crd controlplanes.tenancy.kubeflex.io\n```",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm uninstall kubeflex -n kubeflex-system",
+          "kubectl delete namespace kubeflex-system",
+          "kubectl delete crd controlplanes.tenancy.kubeflex.io"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade KubeFlex",
+        "description": "Update the Helm chart and upgrade:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex -n kubeflex-system\nkubectl get pods -n kubeflex-system\n```\n> Existing control planes will continue running during the upgrade.",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm repo update",
+          "helm upgrade kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex -n kubeflex-system",
+          "kubectl get pods -n kubeflex-system"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Control plane stuck in Provisioning",
+        "description": "**Cause:** Insufficient resources or RBAC issues preventing API server creation.\n\n**Fix:** Check the KubeFlex controller logs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflex -n kubeflex-system\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Cannot access tenant API server",
+        "description": "**Cause:** The control plane service may not be exposed or the kubeconfig secret is not yet created.\n\n**Fix:** Wait for the control plane to reach Ready state, then retry:\n```bash\nkubectl get controlplanes -w\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "You should see the KubeFlex controller running in kubeflex-system namespace and be able to create ControlPlane resources that provision dedicated API servers per tenant.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm install kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex --namespace kubeflex-system --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl get controlplanes",
+        "kubectl get pods -l kubeflex.io/control-plane=tenant-1"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "multi-tenancy",
+      "control-plane",
+      "kubestellar",
+      "isolation"
+    ],
+    "platform": "kubeflex",
+    "platformType": "multi-tenancy",
+    "platformProvider": "KubeStellar",
+    "platformVersions": [
+      "v0.7",
+      "v0.8"
+    ],
+    "supportedK8sVersions": [
+      "1.28",
+      "1.29",
+      "1.30",
+      "1.31"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "ControlPlane",
+      "Namespace",
+      "Deployment",
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "containerImages": [
+      "ghcr.io/kubestellar/kubeflex/controller:latest"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://kubestellar.io/docs/kubeflex/",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kubestellar/kubeflex"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 95
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.28",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "cloudCLI": "none",
+    "resources": "Minimum 2 vCPU, 2GB RAM for the controller + 1 vCPU, 1GB per control plane",
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with Helm 3 installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-16T22:00:00.000Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "platform-install-gen-2.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
